work_completed: Reconciled a stale + dirty local clone: local main was 4 commits behind origin/main (the OTOI 1.0.0 -> 1.0.2 governance upgrade, PR #15) and carried uncommitted README edits. Fast-forwarded main to origin/main and moved the edits onto branch docs/toi-npm-receipts.

Kept the npm distribution-receipt notes, sanitized for this PUBLIC repo: README.md gains an 'npm distribution' subsection and packages/toi/README.md a one-line publication note, stating @neurolift-technologies/toi is on npm at 1.0.0 (Apache-2.0) and @neurolift-technologies/asfdk is not yet published.
Removed internal valuation-evidence framing (E4, 'Memo v1.1 language', 'public-access pending') that the original draft would have published publicly.
decisions_made: Per Joshua, sanitized the notes rather than committing them verbatim, since nlt-toi is public and the draft referenced the confidential 2026-06-19 valuation evidence pass.
Delivered via PR off the now-current main (OTOI 1.0.2).
handoff_record.next_agent_notes: Content-only docs change. nlt-toi has an accessibility-check workflow that has flaked on docs PRs in sibling repos (nlt-otoi); if it fails here it is likely the same non-content issue, not these edits. The local clone is now in sync with origin/main (OTOI 1.0.2).
tests_run: git diff --stat (2 README files, sanitized additions only)
